topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The phone lay on the kitchen counter, its screen glowing with a profile picture I didn't recognize. My breath hitched, a sharp, shallow intake of air that seemed to seize my chest. I picked it up, my fingers trembling so violently I nearly dropped it. It was a private account, clearly hidden, and the follower count was… alarming. A tidal wave of panic washed over me, starting in my stomach and radiating outward. I scanned the recent posts—photos of parties I didn't know about, captions dripping with coded language. My jaw tightened, a knot of sheer disbelief forming in my throat. I needed to know everything, and the sheer volume of hidden life unfolding in my teenager’s digital shadow was overwhelming. I paced the length of the dining room, the floorboards groaning under my frantic steps, unable to focus on anything but the evidence staring back at me.

[story 2]

"Liam, can you just show me your phone?" The words came out tight, brittle. He looked up, his eyes wide with a practiced, almost bored nonchalance that instantly shattered my composure. When he reluctantly handed over the device, I didn't even look at the screen initially. I just stared at his face, searching for any flicker of truth behind the practiced mask. Then I saw it—a secret world. The cascade of notifications, the carefully curated images, the hidden comments. A sudden, hot flush spread across my neck and cheeks. My hands started shaking again, not from cold, but from a desperate need to control the chaos swirling inside my head. I started talking, my voice rising in pitch, stumbling over words as I tried to process the sheer scope of what I had stumbled upon.

[story 3]

I found the login credentials tucked inside a misplaced textbook. The moment I typed them in, the screen lit up with an entire, vibrant universe I never knew existed. My vision tunneled. My heart hammered against my ribs with a frantic, uneven rhythm. Every image, every cryptic message, hit me like a physical blow. I dropped the phone onto the rug, the sound muffled by the sudden, sharp intake of my own breath. I stood there, rigid, arms crossed so tightly my knuckles were white, unable to form a coherent sentence. My mind raced, cycling through possibilities—betrayal, secrecy, an entire identity I was completely unaware of. The air in the room felt thick, suffocating, and I couldn't seem to draw a full breath.
